name: How do I fix uneven floors before installation?

mainEntity.name: How are uneven floors fixed before new flooring is installed?
mainEntity.text: How are uneven floors fixed before new flooring is installed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Uneven floors need to be corrected before new flooring goes down because most materials require a certain level of flatness. The fix depends on the surface and the severity of the issue. High spots may be sanded or ground down. Low spots may be filled with leveling compound. Loose wood subfloors may need additional fasteners or panel replacement. The contractor should determine whether the problem is cosmetic or structural. A slightly uneven area might only need leveling, but a floor with movement, soft spots, or framing issues needs more extensive repair. Floating floors, glue-down floors, and tile each have different flatness tolerances, so the standard is driven by the product being installed. If you are getting estimates, ask how the installer measures flatness and what corrective work is included. Skipping this step is one of the most common reasons new floors fail early or feel wrong underfoot.
acceptedAnswer.description: Uneven floors are usually corrected with sanding, grinding, plywood replacement, fastening, or leveling compound, depending on the type of subfloor and the size of the problem. The floor must meet the manufacturer’s flatness requirements before installation.
